physical.distinguishing_features: Gold ceremonial arm cuff. Mane braided with bone and stone fragments from the First Land.

attacks.name: Claws
attacks.detail: Natural weapons; can be used for unarmed strikes, dealing slashing instead of bludgeoning.

rage.label: Rage
rage.description: +2 damage · B/P/S Resistance · Advantage on STR checks & saves · 1 minute

features.name: Rage
features.description: Bonus action. While raging: advantage on STR checks and STR saving throws; +2 damage to melee attacks using STR; resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage. Lasts 1 minute. 2 uses / long rest.

features.name: Unarmored Defense
features.description: While not wearing armor, AC = 10 + DEX mod (+2) + CON mod (+3) = 15. You may still use a shield.

features.name: Claws
features.description: Natural weapons. You can use your claws to make unarmed strikes; on a hit they deal slashing damage equal to 1d4 + STR modifier.

features.name: Daunting Roar
features.description: Bonus action. Creatures of your choice within 10 ft that can hear you must succeed on a WIS save (DC 8 + Prof + CON mod = 13) or be frightened until the end of your next turn. Recharge: short or long rest.

features.name: Darkvision
features.description: You can see in dim light within 60 ft as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

features.name: Wanderer
features.description: Excellent memory for maps and geography. You can always recall the general layout of terrain, settlements, and other features. You can find food and fresh water for yourself and up to 5 companions each day (if the land offers it).

features.name: Subclass — Zealot (Planned)
features.description: Planned path at level 3. Sekhmet's rage is sacred judgment: destiny-driven, hard to kill, and fueled by violation of something holy.

traits.personality: He watches over companions as if they were a litter of newborn cubs. He does not show it openly — but he positions himself between danger and the weak without thinking.
traits.ideal: Glory is remembrance. To be remembered is to resist erasure. Every battle must mean something — his name must echo in story long after his bones bleach in the sand.
traits.bond: He walks so the memory of the First Land does not die. The weight of a fallen pride lives in every step.
traits.flaw: He is slow to trust outsiders. Respect must be earned in sweat and blood. You do not simply enter a pride by standing near it.

backstory.summary: Before the Temporal Compression, his homeland breathed with mythic air. The horizon was wider. Spirits walked the surface of things. Then the world hardened. Matter thickened. What moved like wind became stone-bound. His pride faded — some into legend, some into the petrified ruins that scholars now mistake for ancient monuments. He held form. Not because he was strongest — but because his will refused to dissolve. He walks a world smaller than it should be, carrying the memory of everything that was lost when the mythic gave way to the physical.

physical.distinguishing_features: Wings. Carries a pressed-flower spellbook at all times. Easily underestimated.

attacks.name: Fire Bolt
attacks.detail: Ranged spell attack. Range 120 ft. Cantrip.

attacks.name: Chill Touch
attacks.detail: Range 120 ft. Target can't regain HP until start of your next turn. Cantrip.

attacks.name: Ray of Frost
attacks.detail: Range 60 ft. Target speed reduced by 10 ft until start of your next turn. Cantrip.

attacks.name: Thunderclap
attacks.detail: CON Save DC 11. Each creature within 5 ft. Cantrip.

attacks.name: Druidcraft
attacks.detail: Cantrip. Simple nature magic — predict weather, light/snuff small flames, harmless effects.

cantrips.name: Fire Bolt
cantrips.description: Ranged spell attack. Hit: 1d10 fire damage. Scales at 5th, 11th, and 17th level.

cantrips.name: Chill Touch
cantrips.description: Ranged spell attack. Hit: 1d8 necrotic. Target can't regain HP until start of your next turn. Undead also have disadvantage on attacks against you.

cantrips.name: Ray of Frost
cantrips.description: Ranged spell attack. Hit: 1d8 cold damage. Target speed -10 ft until start of your next turn.

cantrips.name: Thunderclap
cantrips.description: CON save DC 11. Each creature within 5 ft takes 1d6 thunder on a failed save. Audible 100 ft.

cantrips.name: Druidcraft
cantrips.description: Predict weather, create harmless sensory effects, light/snuff small flames, wilt or cause to bloom a plant no larger than 1 cubic foot.

spells_prepared.name: Catapult
spells_prepared.description: Choose an object weighing 1–5 lbs. It flies up to 90 ft in a straight line, then falls. Each creature in its path makes a DEX save or takes 3d8 bludgeoning. +1d8 per slot level above 1st.

spells_prepared.name: Witch Bolt
spells_prepared.description: Ranged spell attack. Hit: 1d12 lightning. On subsequent turns, use action to deal 1d12 automatically while in range. Ends if target is out of range or you use action for something else. +1d12 per slot level above 1st.

spells_prepared.name: Magic Missile
spells_prepared.description: Create 3 darts of magical force. Each hits a creature of your choice automatically for 1d4+1 force. All darts can target the same creature or different ones. +1 dart per slot level above 1st.

arcane_recovery.label: Arcane Recovery
arcane_recovery.description: Once per day on a Short Rest, recover spell slots totaling ≤ half wizard level (rounded up). At Level 1: recover one 1st-level slot. Cannot recover 6th level or higher.

features.name: Arcane Recovery
features.description: Once per day on a Short Rest, recover spell slots totaling ≤ half wizard level (rounded up). At Level 1: recover one 1st-level slot. Cannot recover 6th level or higher.

features.name: Wizard Spellbook
features.description: Contains 6 prepared 1st-level spells. Gain 2 new spells per wizard level. Copy from scrolls and tomes (2 hrs + 50 gp/spell level). Spellbook is a pressed-flower diary that also happens to contain the secrets of arcane reality.

features.name: Fairy Flight
features.description: Has a flying speed equal to walking speed (10 ft). Cannot fly while wearing medium or heavy armor.

features.name: Fairy Magic
features.description: Can cast Druidcraft cantrip for free. At level 3: cast Faerie Fire once per long rest. At level 5: cast Enlarge/Reduce once per long rest.

features.name: Fey Ancestry
features.description: Advantage on saving throws against being charmed. Magic cannot put her to sleep.

features.name: Witherbloom Initiate
features.description: Learned to weave life and death energies. Gains the Druidcraft cantrip and two 1st-level spells from the Druid or Wizard spell list. Life and death magic comes naturally to her — the swamp taught her that nothing truly ends.

traits.personality: Small in stature, enormous in presence. Her curiosity is insatiable — she will touch everything, ask about everything, remember everything. Her laughter is genuine; her silences are calculating.
traits.ideal: Knowledge without application is just hoarding. She studies the boundary between life and death not out of morbidity, but because she believes something important lives there — something the world is pretending not to notice.
traits.bond: Her spellbook contains pressed flowers, margin notes in three languages, and a spell she hasn't been able to cast yet. The people she travels with have become, unexpectedly, something she would cross swamps for.
traits.flaw: She occasionally forgets that other people cannot fly, hide instantly, or talk to mushrooms — which leads to plans that only work if you're her. She also underestimates her own size in situations requiring physical presence.

backstory.summary: She arrived at Witherbloom with pressed flowers already in her spellbook and a question she hadn't told anyone yet. The swamp suited her — it didn't apologize for being complicated, for smelling of both rot and bloom at once. It studied the same question she did: where exactly does life end and death begin, and is anything actually living in that gap? She is very small. She compensates with extraordinary thoroughness. Her notes are annotated. Her annotations have annotations. Her spellbook has a table of contents and an index.

physical.distinguishing_features: Shadow-lined pouch of carefully wrapped trinkets. Moves like she was never in the room.

attacks.name: Shortsword
attacks.detail: Melee Weapon Attack. Finesse, light. Primary blade.

attacks.name: Dagger
attacks.detail: Melee or Ranged (20/60 ft). Finesse, light, thrown. Carries two.

attacks.name: Shortbow
attacks.detail: Ranged Weapon Attack. Range 80/320 ft. 20 arrows carried.

attacks.name: Crowbar
attacks.detail: Improvised weapon. Also grants advantage on STR checks to force open doors and containers.

sneak_attack.label: Sneak Attack
sneak_attack.description: Once per turn. Extra 1d6 damage on a hit with a finesse or ranged weapon if you have advantage, or an ally is adjacent to the target and you don't have disadvantage.

blessing_of_the_raven_queen.label: Blessing of the Raven Queen
blessing_of_the_raven_queen.description: Bonus action: teleport up to 30 ft to an unoccupied space you can see. Uses = proficiency bonus. At level 3, gain resistance to all damage until the start of your next turn when you teleport.

features.name: Expertise
features.description: Proficiency bonus doubled for two chosen skills or one skill and Thieves' Tools. Raven's expertise: Stealth, Sleight of Hand, Deception, Acrobatics — the full suite of someone who was never where you thought she was.

features.name: Sneak Attack
features.description: Once per turn, deal extra 1d6 damage with a finesse or ranged weapon when you have advantage on the attack roll, or when an ally is within 5 ft of the target and you don't have disadvantage. Scales with rogue level.

features.name: Thieves' Cant
features.description: A secret language of dialect, jargon, and code woven into ordinary conversation. Also grants knowledge of criminal signs and symbols — safe houses, territorial markers, danger warnings.

features.name: Blessing of the Raven Queen
features.description: Bonus action: teleport up to 30 ft to any unoccupied space in sight. Uses = proficiency bonus (2) per long rest. At level 3, becomes ghostly and translucent, gaining resistance to all damage until the start of her next turn after teleporting.

features.name: Darkvision
features.description: See in dim light as bright light, and in darkness as dim light, within 60 ft. Can't discern color in darkness — only shades of grey.

features.name: Fey Ancestry
features.description: Advantage on saving throws against being charmed. Magic cannot put her to sleep.

features.name: Necrotic Resistance
features.description: Resistance to necrotic damage.

features.name: Trance
features.description: Does not sleep. Meditates for 4 hours instead, gaining the benefit of a full 8-hour rest.

features.name: Criminal Contact
features.description: A reliable network contact who acts as liaison to a broader criminal web. Can send and receive messages across great distances through local messengers, corrupt caravan masters, and seedy sailors.

traits.personality: Moves through a room like she was never in it. Helpful to the party in ways that are difficult to trace back to her. Denies taking things even when they are visibly in her belt pouch.
traits.ideal: Information and positioning. She is never the most powerful person in the room — she is the one who knows where all the exits are.
traits.bond: The Raven Queen's shadow is long. Raven doesn't talk about why she knows the places between life and death as well as she does. She keeps small things from people she trusts — never admits it.
traits.flaw: If it catches the light, she wants it. She is aware this is a problem. She has decided it is not a problem.

carried.name: Crowbar
carried.description: Advantage on STR checks to force open things. Also a weapon if it comes to it.

backstory.summary: She doesn't explain the Shadowfell. She doesn't explain the Raven Queen. She doesn't explain the gap between what she was before and what she is now. She carries small things from people she's lost — organized carefully, wrapped in cloth, hidden in a shadow-lined pouch. She remembers where each one came from. Some are trophies. Some are tied to contracts she doesn't talk about. She joined this party for reasons that made sense at the time and has since stopped examining too closely.

physical.distinguishing_features: Bronze draconic scales. No hair. Carries Cartographer's Tools from his archival days at Moogle & Co.

attacks.name: Mace
attacks.detail: Melee Weapon Attack. The preferred instrument of divine correction.

cantrips.name: Toll the Dead
cantrips.description: A creature you can see must pass a Wisdom save or take 1d8 necrotic damage, or 1d12 if it's missing any hit points. Scales at 5th, 11th, and 17th level.

cantrips.name: Word of Radiance
cantrips.description: Each creature of your choice within 5 ft must succeed on a CON save or take 1d6 radiant damage. Scales at 5th, 11th, and 17th level.

cantrips.name: Guidance
cantrips.description: Touch one willing creature. Once before the spell ends, the target adds 1d4 to one ability check of its choice.

spells_prepared.name: Bless
spells_prepared.description: Bless up to 3 creatures. Each adds 1d4 to attack rolls and saving throws. +1 target per slot level above 1st.

spells_prepared.name: Cure Wounds
spells_prepared.description: A touched creature regains 1d8 + WIS modifier HP. No effect on undead or constructs. +1d8 per slot level above 1st.

spells_prepared.name: Fog Cloud
spells_prepared.description: 20-foot-radius sphere of fog, heavily obscured. +20 ft. radius per slot level above 1st. [Tempest Domain]

spells_prepared.name: Guiding Bolt
spells_prepared.description: Ranged spell attack. Hit: 4d6 radiant damage. Next attack against the target has advantage. +1d6 per slot level above 1st.

spells_prepared.name: Healing Word
spells_prepared.description: A visible creature regains 1d4 + WIS modifier HP. No effect on undead or constructs. +1d4 per slot level above 1st.

spells_prepared.name: Thunderwave
spells_prepared.description: Each creature in a 15-ft cube makes a CON save. Fail: 2d8 thunder damage, pushed 10 ft. Success: half damage. Audible 300 ft. +1d8 per slot level above 1st. [Tempest Domain]

wrath_of_the_storm.label: Wrath of the Storm
wrath_of_the_storm.description: Reaction when hit by a creature within 5 ft. Deal 2d8 lightning or thunder damage (DEX save DC 13 halves). Uses = WIS modifier.

features.name: Bonus Proficiencies
features.description: Proficiency with martial weapons and heavy armor. Evo walked in expecting a desk job. He got a war portfolio instead.

features.name: Wrath of the Storm
features.description: Reaction triggered when a creature within 5 ft hits you. It takes 2d8 lightning or thunder damage (DEX save DC 13 halves). Uses = WIS modifier (3) per long rest.

features.name: Domain Spells
features.description: Fog Cloud and Thunderwave are always prepared and don't count against prepared spell limit. Higher-level domain spells unlock as Evo levels.

features.name: Lightning Breath Weapon
features.description: Exhale a 5×30 ft. line of lightning as an action. DC = 8 + CON mod + Prof bonus (12). Each creature makes a DEX save, taking 2d6 lightning on a failure or half on success. 1 use per short or long rest.

features.name: Lightning Damage Resistance
features.description: Resistance to lightning damage.

features.name: Inside Informant
features.description: Spent time in the Moogle & Co. archives. Has catalogued a network of corporate partners and rivals, intelligence he intends to leverage for Duck Duck Dragon — his as-yet-unfunded information startup.

traits.personality: Radiates the quiet confidence of someone who definitely belongs here and absolutely did not misread the job posting.
traits.ideal: Information is currency. The right leverage at the right moment is worth more than any spell slot.
traits.bond: Duck Duck Dragon will be built. The startup needs capital, the party needs a healer — for now, interests align.
traits.flaw: Lawful Evil wrapped in a support role. He will absolutely sell you out if the market conditions are right.

carried.name: Cartographer's Tools
carried.description: Tool proficiency. Holdover from the archives.

backstory.summary: Saw a job ad for Moogle & Co., a Fortune 5000 firm. Got hired on the spot. Misread the posting — they wanted a clerk, not a cleric. The office never let him forget it. He spent his internship in the archives instead, quietly mapping their entire partner network. Now he's building Duck Duck Dragon, an information brokerage, and needs startup capital. Joining a random adventuring party seemed like the most efficient path to funding.
backstory.verbatim: A lowly intern. I saw a job ad for a big fortune 5000 company called moogle & got hired on the spot. But I read the job description wrong, they didn't want a cleric they wanted a clerc(k). They all made fun of me for that. I used my time in the archives to learn most of their partners and I aim to undercut them in the global markets by starting my own information company called duck duck dragon. I need money for my startup so i joined a random party to get funds.

physical.distinguishing_features: Self-proclaimed Wall God. Always visibly excited when near a structurally significant wall.

bonus_actions: Second Wind — 2 / Long Rest: regain 1d10 + 1 HP; regain one use on a Short Rest
special: Tiny Clockwork Device: Activate whenever you or another creature activates it with a touch; the device produces the effect chosen at the time of creation.

cantrips.name: Mending
cantrips.description: Repairs a single break or tear in an object you touch, such as a broken chain link, two halves of a broken key, or a torn cloak. Does not restore magic to a magic item.

cantrips.name: Prestidigitation
cantrips.description: Minor magical trick: create a small sensory effect, light or snuff a candle, clean or soil an object, chill/warm/flavor up to 1 cubic foot of material, make a symbol appear on a surface, or produce a small trinket.

second_wind.label: Second Wind
second_wind.notes: Regain one use on a Short Rest.

features.name: Gnomish Cunning
features.description: Advantage on INT, WIS, and CHA saving throws against magic.

features.name: Darkvision
features.description: Darkvision 60 ft.

features.name: Tiny Clockwork Device
features.description: Activate whenever you or another creature activates it with a touch; it produces the effect chosen at the time of creation.

features.name: Second Wind
features.description: Bonus Action: regain 1d10 + 1 HP. 2 uses per long rest; regain one use on a short rest.

traits.personality: Bright-eyed and excited to discuss any sufficiently sturdy-looking wall. Believes himself to be of divinity walking among mortals.
traits.ideal: The greatest wall ever built will be his temple. Every structure he encounters is evaluated for its potential.
traits.bond: His name means Strong Wall. He intends to live up to it — literally.
traits.flaw: Cannot pass a notable wall without stopping to examine it, regardless of circumstances.

backstory.summary: Malc Grann — meaning Strong Wall — is what this gnome calls himself. Self-proclaimed Wall God. Believes himself to be of divinity walking among mortals. Bright-eyed and excited to discuss and examine any sufficiently sturdy-looking wall. His dream is to build the biggest and best wall anyone has ever seen and declare it his temple.
backstory.verbatim: Malc Grann, meaning Strong Wall, is what this Gnome calls himself. Self proclaimed "Wall God". Believes himself to be of divinity walking among mortals. Bright eyed and excited to discuss and examine any sufficiently sturdy looking wall. His dream is to build the biggest and best wall that anyone has ever seen and declare it his temple!

traits.personality: I've read every book in the world's greatest libraries and in almost every plane in existence. There's nothing I like more than a good mystery.
traits.ideal: Knowledge. The path to power and self-improvement is through knowledge. (Neutral)
traits.bond: I've been searching my whole life for the answer to a certain question. Where is my brother, the crown prince. Hiroe Signal of kamigawa neon dynasty
traits.flaw: Once I am fixated on learning or reading, I tend to seclude myself in my own world and ignore my surroundings.
